sourcePrecedence: blueprint/phase-1-the-red-flag-gate.md#1E. Submit normalization and synchronous safety engine
forbiddenValidationTonePhrases: check the form and try again

transitionRules: urgent_diversion_required may render immediately after SafetyDecisionRecord settles urgent, but urgent_diverted is forbidden until UrgentDiversionSettlement(settlementState = issued).
screen_clear and residual_risk_flagged both render through safe receipt grammar, but residual_risk_flagged must keep review-sensitive wording.
failed_safe is a settlement/result posture, not a calm receipt alias and not a field-validation state.

variants.title: Your request has been sent
variants.summary: We have placed your request in the routine review path.
supportingBullets: Use the reference code if you need to contact the practice.
The status page will update in the same shell when new information is ready.
variants.liveRegionMessage: Your request has been sent. You can now view the status.

variants.title: Your request has been sent for review
variants.summary: A clinician will review the detail you sent before the routine next step is chosen.
supportingBullets: Keep your phone available if you asked us to contact you today.
The status page will continue to show the same request lineage.
variants.liveRegionMessage: Your request has been sent for review.

variants.title: Get urgent help now
variants.summary: This request cannot stay in the routine queue.
supportingBullets: Call 999 now if the person is in immediate danger, has severe chest pain, or is struggling to breathe.
If you cannot call yourself, ask someone nearby to help you now.
variants.liveRegionMessage: Urgent help is needed now. This request cannot stay in the routine queue.
forbiddenPhrases: check the form and try again

secondaryAction.label: See why we changed the path

variants.title: Urgent guidance has been issued
variants.summary: We have switched this request to the urgent pathway and recorded that change.
supportingBullets: Use the urgent guidance now. Do not wait for a routine reply.
The urgent pathway stays attached to the same request lineage for audit and continuity.
variants.liveRegionMessage: Urgent guidance has been issued for this request.
forbiddenPhrases: check the form and try again

variants.title: We could not safely complete this online
variants.summary: Your details are still available, but this request was not placed in the routine queue.
supportingBullets: Call the practice now so a person can decide the next safe step.
If the problem is getting worse, use urgent help instead of waiting.
variants.liveRegionMessage: We could not safely complete this online. Call the practice now.

gapResolutions.summary: The rulebook, state machine, and copy contract now keep urgent_diversion_required separate from urgent_diverted and require UrgentDiversionSettlement before the issued state may render.
The synchronous safety gate is now frozen as schema-backed rule metadata plus authored decision tables rather than prose-only spreadsheet logic.
Unresolved attachment meaning or parser disagreement now routes into fail-closed review or failed-safe recovery instead of silently dropping to screen_clear.
Hard-stop rules remain dominant and cannot be softened away by calibrators or soft-score smoothing.
Urgent outcome copy now reads as an unmistakable pathway change with one dominant next action instead of a form-validation or generic error surface.
Safe receipt and failed-safe recovery now have different state names, different primary actions, and non-overlapping copy identifiers.

assumptions.summary: Rule metadata uses the placeholder roles already frozen in the DCB0129 and signoff packs; named approvers remain a later governance substitution, not a semantic change.
Identity calibrators remain mandatory for Phase 1 until adjudicated challenge data meets the published graduation criteria per stratum.

risks.summary: Urgent text that sounds like generic failure or form validation would cause unsafe hesitation.
risks.mitigation: Urgent variants explicitly ban validation tone, surface a single dominant action, and use pathway-change language only.

risks.summary: Soft-score changes could drift if challenge-set diagnostics are not carried with the rule-pack version.
risks.mitigation: The rule pack publishes harm-ratio thresholds, calibrator discipline, and signed-off diagnostics fields together.

conflicts.summary: Later clinical governance may add challenge cases, named approvers, or stricter handoff routing, but it may not weaken the Phase 1 rule IDs, hard-stop dominance, or outcome semantics frozen here.
